title: IDF Drone Strike Kills Brothers in Beit Lahiya, Northern Gaza
detail: Israeli forces launched a drone strike in Beit Lahiya, northern Gaza, killing brothers Abdelmalek and Abdel Sattar al-Attar. Witnesses said the strike fell outside the zone under Israeli military control under the October 2025 ceasefire agreement. Civil Defence spokesman Mahmoud Bassal confirmed the deaths. The attack was the first of three separate Israeli killing incidents on Thursday, April 16, in Gaza.

media.caption: IDF drone kills brothers in Beit Lahiya, northern Gaza — April 16, 2026

title: Nine-Year-Old Saleh Badawi Shot Dead by Israeli Forces in Zeitoun, Gaza City
detail: Israeli forces shot and killed nine-year-old Saleh Badawi in the Zeitoun neighborhood east of Gaza City. The child's death is among the most documented casualties of the second day of Israeli attacks on April 16. Gaza's Government Media Office said the total number of ceasefire violations had reached 2,400 since October 2025. The killing drew particular condemnation from humanitarian organizations tracking child casualties.

media.caption: Nine-year-old Palestinian child killed by Israeli fire in Zeitoun, Gaza City

title: Israeli Forces Kill Mohsen al-Dabbari South of Khan Younis
detail: Mohsen al-Dabbari, 38, was killed by Israeli fire south of Khan Younis on April 16, 2026. The killing is one of three separate Israeli shooting incidents documented by Al Jazeera's Civil Defence sources in Gaza on April 16, continuing a pattern of near-daily Israeli operations inside Gaza despite the October 2025 ceasefire. Separate fire toward homes and displaced persons' tents east of Maghazi refugee camp wounded three people including a teenage boy.

media.caption: Israeli forces kill Palestinian south of Khan Younis as ceasefire violations continue

title: Israeli Settlers Increasingly Emboldened as Protection Efforts Prove Insufficient
detail: Reports indicate that Israeli settlers in the West Bank have become more emboldened in their activities, with protective presence activism by Palestinian rights groups proving insufficient to deter settler attacks and harassment. The escalation comes amid ongoing tensions over settlement expansion and land disputes in occupied Palestinian territories.

media.caption: Report on escalating settler activities in West Bank

title: Israelis and Palestinians Hold Joint Rally Against West Bank Settler Attacks
detail: In a rare display of cross-community solidarity, Israelis and Palestinians came together to rally against settler attacks in the West Bank. The joint demonstration represents an unusual moment of cooperation between the two communities, typically divided by the ongoing conflict, as they unite against what they perceive as extremist settler violence targeting Palestinian civilians.

media.caption: Coverage of joint Israeli-Palestinian rally against settler violence
